name: InMed Pharmaceuticals Inc.
business: We are a pharmaceutical company developing a pipeline of proprietary small molecule drug candidates that are preferential signaling ligands of the endogenous CB1 and CB2 receptors as well as other receptor targets linked to human disease. Together with our wholly owned subsidiary, BayMedica, LLC, we also have significant know-how in developing proprietary manufacturing approaches to produce and sell bulk rare cannabinoids as ingredients for various market sectors.
sellsTo: Health and Wellness sector (B2B bulk rare cannabinoids)

exposures.sentence: We are a pharmaceutical company developing a pipeline of proprietary small molecule drug candidates that are preferential signaling ligands of the endogenous CB1 and CB2 receptors as well as other receptor targets linked to human disease.

BayMedica’s commercial business specializes in the B2B commercialization of bulk rare, non-intoxicating cannabinoids as raw materials for the Health and Wellness sector that are bioidentical to those found in nature.

tamClaims.claim: 6.9 million Americans are living with AD, expected to grow to 12.7 million by 2050.
tamClaims.sentence: It’s estimated that 6.9 million Americans are living with AD, and it’s expected to grow to 12.7 million by 2050.

tamClaims.claim: Health and long-term care costs for AD valued at $360 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: The disease has a major medical and societal burden with health and long-term care costs valued at $360 billion.

tamClaims.claim: 196 million people worldwide live with age-related macular degeneration.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the World Health Organization, 196 million people worldwide live with age-related macular degeneration.

tamClaims.claim: 19.8 million Americans aged 40+ suffer from AMD (12.6% of the population).
tamClaims.sentence: An estimated 19.8M Americans aged 40+, about 12.6% of the population, suffer from AMD.
